column8: – You (telephone) for ages! You really (not finish)? – I (not get) through yet. I am trying to get to our Paris office but the line (be) engaged all morning.
column9: He (play) the piano since six o’clock in the morning. He only just (stop).
column10: Why you (not bring) me the letters? You (not type) them yet?
column11: – Are you going to sunbathe? – Don’t be ridiculous! It (rain) all day.
column12: – What you (do) up to now? – Nothing special, but I (cook) dinner.
column13: The police (not find) the burglar yet. They (look) for him since Saturday.
column14: Ann (fail) her exam three times because she is so bad at doing sums. But she (practise) for a week now, I hope she will pass it in the end.
column15: – What (happen) to the fridge? – Something (go) wrong with it.
column16: I know him well. I (know) him since our childhood.
column17: – How long you (have) these gloves? – I (have) them for years.
